name: Back Squat (bodyweight ratio)
notes: Horsemen benchmark is 2.5×BW. SS novice completion is 1.5×BW. Female standards reflect strength-to-weight ratios adjusted for average sex-based lean mass distribution.

name: Deadlift (bodyweight ratio)
notes: Horsemen target is 2.5×BW. Elite 3.0×BW is world-class powerlifting territory. Tactical standards typically target the advanced tier (2.5×BW).

name: Strict Press (bodyweight ratio)
notes: Horsemen benchmark is 1×BW military press. SS novice completion is 0.75×BW. One of the most honest upper-body strength tests as no momentum assistance is permitted.

name: Bench Press (bodyweight ratio)
notes: Horsemen benchmark is 1.5×BW. Horsemen PT Test II includes 10×BW bench press (10 reps at bodyweight), which is a strength endurance variant.

name: Power Clean (bodyweight ratio)
notes: Horsemen Olympic day workouts use 80–90% 1RM in wave patterns. Power clean is a key SS program exercise at intermediate level.

name: Pull-ups (max unweighted reps)
notes: Horsemen PT standard is 15+ strict pull-ups. Horsemen PT Test II requires 15 reps at +25# added weight — use pull_up_weighted_bw_ratio for that standard. Female standards reflect body composition differences in upper-body lean mass.

name: Weighted Pull-up 1RM (added load as BW ratio)
notes: Horsemen Riley Black protocol includes weighted pull-up ladders at 85%/90%/95% of 1RM. PT Test II standard of 15 reps at +25# is a strength endurance target, not a 1RM; map to pull_up_reps for that test.

name: 3-Mile Run (time in minutes)
notes: Horsemen performance benchmark is sub-20:00 (male). PT test standard is sub-22:30. PT Test II includes a 5-mile run standard (sub-35:00). Female thresholds adjusted proportionally by typical sex-based aerobic capacity differences (~15%).

name: Secret Service Snatch Test (SSST) — Reps in 10 Minutes
notes: Horsemen documentation: 200+ reps = Team Guy Standard; 250 = "ridiculous"; 275 = record. Female standard uses 16kg (35 lb) bell. This test combines aerobic, anaerobic, grip, and mental endurance in a single 10-minute window — it is both a benchmark and a training tool. SSST prep requires progressive KB snatch volume work over 4–8 weeks before testing.

name: 2km Row (time in minutes)
notes: Horsemen workouts include 500m row sub-1:30 + 400m run as a power cardiovascular benchmark (maps to ~6:00/2k pace territory). CrossFit uses 2k row as a standard benchmark. Gym Jones uses rowing extensively for power endurance testing. Female standards adjusted for typical sex-based aerobic and mass differences.

name: 1-Mile Run (time in minutes)
notes: BJJ competitor minimum prerequisite is sub-10:00 (1 mile ≤ 10 min). Used as an aerobic readiness gate — athletes who cannot run a sub-10 mile lack the aerobic base to sustain hard rolling without excessive cardiac drift. Female standards adjusted for typical sex-based aerobic capacity differences.
